← all tutorials

Consistency Models & CAP — interactive 📖 CONSISTENCY_MODELS.md 📄 consistency_models.py

📖 Full guide (.md, green) — the long-form written explanation.  📄 Source code (.py, orange) — the ground-truth logic this page is verified against.
The contract each read makes: linearizable (no stale reads, costs quorum) down to eventual (fast, may be stale). Click a rung to see what it guarantees & prevents, then open the quorum calculator and drag N/R/W to watch the R+W>N overlap rule flip a read between fresh and stale. The CAP panel shows CP vs AP during a partition. All numbers recomputed in JS from the identical model as consistency_models.py.

① Consistency model hierarchy — click a rung to see guarantees & anomalies

What to do: click a model. Higher = stronger & costlier (more coordination, less available under partition). Stale read is the dividing line: linearizable/sequential prevent it; causal and weaker allow it. Causal is the strongest model that stays available during a partition.
Hierarchy: linearizable > sequential > causal > read-your-writes > eventual. Each stronger model prevents every anomaly the weaker one prevents, plus more.

② CAP tradeoff — during a partition, drop C (CP) or drop A (AP)

What to do: toggle CP vs AP, then toggle the partition. 3 replicas {A,B,C}; a client writes x=1 to the minority {A} during a split. CP errors on the minority (keeps C, drops A); AP accepts everywhere and diverges (keeps A, drops C). PACELC adds: even with no partition you still trade Latency vs Consistency.
fresh / committed value stale / divergent CP block

③ Quorum calculator — drag N / R / W, watch the R+W>N overlap rule

What to do: set N replicas, the write quorum W, and the read quorum R. A read is guaranteed fresh iff R + W > N — the read set and write set must OVERLAP in ≥1 replica. The overlap replicas turn green; write-only gold; read-only blue.

④ Gold check — hierarchy, CAP, and R+W>N recomputed live

What this proves: the page re-runs the model hierarchy, the CAP family split, and the quorum overlap math from the identical data as consistency_models.py GOLD CHECK. The green check: OK below confirms the JS numbers match the .py exactly.
check: … JS hierarchy + CAP families + R+W>N rule match consistency_models.py GOLD CHECK.
